title: senecio rupestris var. calabricus
intro: Senecio rupestris var. calabricus, also known as Calabric Rock Ragwort, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Calabric Rock Ragwort include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Calabric Rock Ragwort can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's flowers and leaves in a topical cream or ointment.
